column1: # Export Layers To Files — CSV Manifest Template
# HOW IT WORKS
# One CSV row = one output image.
#   - A Path that points at a LAYER  exports that layer.
#   - A Path that points at a FOLDER  flattens that whole folder
#     into a single image.
# Nothing else is exported. There is no positional matching and no
# "row count must equal layer count" rule: every row names exactly
# what it targets via its Path column
# layers in Photoshop never misaligns your manifest.
# Anything you name is exported even if it (or a parent group) is
# hidden — putting it in the manifest IS the intent to export it.
# COLUMNS  (Width
# Width   — output canvas width in px.  Empty or 0 = natural size (no resize).
# Height  — output canvas height in px. Empty or 0 = natural size.
#            Width and Height are independent (277
# Padding — px kept around the asset before fitting (fit mode only). Empty = 0.
# Filename — output filename WITHOUT extension. Output-only; unrelated to the
#            layer/folder name. Use subfolder/name to nest (folders are created
#            automatically). Quote the field if the name contains a comma:
#            "name
# Mode    — optional
#            fit    : scale the asset to fit inside (W-Padding*2) x (H-Padding*2)
#                     centered on a W×H canvas.
#            canvas : keep the asset at original px
#                     cropping any overflow. Padding has no effect in canvas mode.
# Path    — REQUIRED. The source locator
column2: read top-down through the Layers panel:

column1: #            Name             a child matched by name (whitespace trimmed).
#            [n]              the n-th direct child
#                             AND sub-groups together exactly as the panel shows.
#            Parent/Child     descend through groups with "/".
#            Parent/[2]/Leaf  names and indices can be mixed.
#            Only a segment that is exactly [digits] is an index — "Symbols [ALL]"
#            is a NAME (the brackets are part of it).
# SCOPE  (optional
column2: on a line BEFORE the header)

column1: #   scope: GroupA
#   Lists one or more TOP-LEVEL groups (by name or [index]). When present
#   row's Path is resolved relative to each scope in turn
#   listed
column2: and each scope writes into its OWN output sub-folder named after it

column1: #   (e.g. OUT/GroupA/...
#   from the document root.
# COMMENTS & BLANK LINES
#   Lines starting with # and blank lines are ignored. Inline comments work too:
#   anything after a # on a line is ignored. The header row is auto-detected.
Width

# A full-size background layer
# A logo scaled to fit a 400×200 box with 20px breathing room
# "fit" is the default

column1: # Canvas set to exactly Width x Height; the asset keeps its original
# pixels (overflow is cropped). Use for pixel-perfect icons/sprites.
# Empty Width/Height exports the asset at its own size (no resize).
# A Path pointing at a GROUP flattens the whole group into one image.
# Size/Mode apply to the flattened result just like a layer.
# Use subfolder/filename in the Filename column to organise outputs.
# Move the line below ABOVE the header to enable it. Each listed group
# becomes its own output sub-folder
